target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.context: Male C57BL/6 mice were fed a compositionally defined diet supplemented with inulin before or during 2.5% DSS exposure; inflammation was assessed using body-weight trajectory, colon length/weight, histopathology, goblet-cell depletion, fecal lipocalin-2, and colonic cytokine readouts compared with cellulose/control diet.

target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.context: Steady-state mice received inulin-containing diet without DSS challenge; the comparison asks whether inulin alone induced colitis-like inflammation before chemical injury, using colon pathology and inflammatory readouts rather than DSS-induced disease severity.

target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.entity_2: fecal bacterial load and pro-inflammatory flagellin activity
question.context: Fecal microbiota from DSS-treated mice was profiled by 16S sequencing, qPCR-based bacterial-load measurement, and HEK-TLR5 flagellin activity assay; the question asks whether inulin increased microbial signals capable of driving innate inflammatory pathways.

target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.context: Before and after DSS exposure, fecal bacterial communities were compared by alpha diversity, phylum-level shifts, and genus-level differential abundance; the expected finding is not a single uniform increase or decrease, but a compositional rearrangement with enriched and depleted taxa.

target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.entity_2: microbial diversity in offspring or DSS-associated microbiota
question.context: Microbial diversity was measured using sequence-based diversity metrics after maternal or direct inulin exposure; the target finding emphasizes loss of diversity in the disease-relevant microbiota context rather than a general prebiotic benefit.

target_paper.title: Inulin aggravates colitis through gut microbiota modulation and MyD88/IL-18 signaling

question.entity_1: antibiotic microbiota depletion or IL-18/MyD88 pathway disruption
question.context: Inulin-fed DSS-treated mice were tested under microbiota-depleted, MyD88-deficient, TLR5/NLRC4-deficient, or IL-18-deficient conditions; the question asks whether removing microbial or inflammasome signaling reduces the extra colitis severity caused by inulin.

target_paper.title: BTLA agonist attenuates Th17-driven inflammation in a mouse model of steroid-resistant asthma

target_paper.title: BTLA agonist attenuates Th17-driven inflammation in a mouse model of steroid-resistant asthma

question.context: Ex vivo Th17-cell treatment was used to test whether activating BTLA induces inhibitory signaling mechanisms that could explain reduced inflammatory output in steroid-resistant asthma.

target_paper.title: Gut bacterium Intestinimonas butyriciproducens improves host metabolic health: evidence from cohort and animal intervention studies

question.context: The target paper combines a human cohort analysis with diet-induced obesity mouse intervention experiments; the question asks whether the bacterium is linked to or causally improves metabolic-health phenotypes rather than merely being taxonomically present.

question.context: Diet-induced obese mice received the bacterium or placebo; blood glucose during insulin tolerance testing was used to assess insulin sensitivity, with lower glucose after insulin challenge indicating improved insulin responsiveness.

target_paper.title: Gut bacterium Intestinimonas butyriciproducens improves host metabolic health: evidence from cohort and animal intervention studies

target_paper.title: Gut bacterium Intestinimonas butyriciproducens improves host metabolic health: evidence from cohort and animal intervention studies

question.context: Diet-induced obese mice were treated with the bacterium or placebo; adiposity and metabolic outcomes were assessed to determine whether the bacterium counteracts obesity-associated fat accumulation.

target_paper.title: A Novel CRISPR/Cas9-mediated Mouse Model of Colon Carcinogenesis

question.context: Mouse colon epithelium was engineered for inducible Cas9 nickase activity with guide RNAs targeting common colorectal cancer genes including Apc, Pten, Trp53, and Smad4; tumor formation was evaluated after dox induction.

question.context: The model was designed as a dox-inducible CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis system rather than a classic Cre-lox conditional allele model; the question asks whether this design reduces reliance on Cre recombinase as the initiating mutational mechanism.

target_paper.title: Microwave ablation combined with immune checkpoint inhibitor enhanced the antitumor immune activation and memory in rechallenged tumor mouse model

question.context: A post-ablation tumor rechallenge mouse model was used to test whether combining microwave ablation with checkpoint blockade controls growth of newly challenged tumors better than monotherapy or control conditions.

target_paper.title: Microwave ablation combined with immune checkpoint inhibitor enhanced the antitumor immune activation and memory in rechallenged tumor mouse model
